title: × heucherella alba
intro: × Heucherella alba, also known as White Foamflower, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Heuchera and Coralbells, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and maple-like leaves. This plant is used in herbal medicine for its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of × Heucherella alba include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a popular choice for topical applications. Herbal preparations of × Heucherella alba typically involve making teas or infusions from the leaves and roots, which can be used to treat various skin conditions and promote overall well-being.
